TextENGNew: The informant -- Sato-san -- had me get in his car and set off.
He favored narrow back streets, making short left turns again and again.
``...Checking for a tail with the left-turn method?``
``Yeah... you know your stuff.``
The left-turn method was one of the oldest tricks in the book for detecting surveillance.@kIt could identify a one-on-one tail.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: ...But real surveillance was conducted by teams, so this classic method was only a stopgap at best.@k...Of course, doing it was still better than not.
``...The boss didn't tell me anything about who you are, Akasaka-kun.@k@vS05/00/104400050. ...You're police, aren't you?@k@vS05/00/104400051. Given who the boss is, that's pretty obvious.``
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: ``If I'm not obligated to answer, I'd rather decline.``
``Good grief...@k@vS05/00/104400053. Ever since the mahjong game, you've been a completely different person!@k@vS05/00/104400054. Where'd the fresh-faced kid from earlier go?``
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: I gave a wry smile.
...Looking back, I had to be grateful they'd made me play mahjong.
It had helped me find my rhythm.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: Eventually, satisfied that no car was following us, Sato-san headed out of the city toward the suburbs.
A pitch-dark rural road where nothing was visible beyond the streetlights.
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: ...The only sounds were the hum of the car's air conditioning and the chorus of frogs and insects.
``...The boss tells me you want to hear about Hinamizawa Village, yeah?``
``About the old families known as the Three Families who control the village, or that the Sonozaki family currently holds absolute power -- that sort of thing?``
``...If you know that much already, I'll skip the preamble.``
``Then, please.@k@vS05/13/101300383. ...Can you confirm or deny whether the Onigafuchi Defense Alliance is involved in the kidnapping of Minister Inukai's grandson?``
Sato-san checked the rearview mirror one more time to confirm no suspicious cars were following, then began to speak.
``...Last night, a family council was convened at the main Sonozaki estate.@k@vS05/00/104400058. You know what a family council is... did the boss explain?``
``An ordinary family council would just be relatives getting together over a cup of tea.@k@vS05/00/104400060. But a Sonozaki family council is something else entirely.``
A family council held by the Sonozaki main house.
...Nothing less than a summit of those who truly ruled Hinamizawa Village.
No casual family chit-chat -- this was about the village.
About the anti-dam resistance.@kAbout everything else -- the gathering that decided, for all intents and purposes, the very fate of the village.
At the apex of the council sat Oryou Sonozaki.
...Called 'Empress Sonozaki' by some, she was the supreme elder, feared and revered in equal measure.
...Infirm with age and frequently bedridden, her words nonetheless still carried enough weight to shape the village's destiny.
Lately her strength had been declining, and on bad days she remained confined to her futon.
When councils were held during such times, Oryou participated while still lying in bed.
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: ...In the center of a stately Japanese-style room, propped up only from the waist, her face stern even from within her futon -- that was Oryou Sonozaki herself...
Seated at her side was the next head of the family: Mion Sonozaki.
Still young... no, 'young' didn't even do justice to it.@kShe was a girl who still bore traces of childhood.
Her role was merely to sit beside Oryou, occasionally relaying messages at the elder's behest, but...
She was the one and only person deemed worthy of succeeding Oryou.
Possessing the same hawk-like eyes, a gaze alone that could freeze the hearts of those who met it... the granddaughter of the clan head, upon whom great expectations rested.
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: And flanking them on both sides sat several elders from the other two families -- the Kimiyoshi and the Furude.
The Kimiyoshi representative was, of course, the current village chief, Kiichirou Kimiyoshi.
...Lined up beside him were several more of his direct relatives.
On the opposite side sat the remaining family, the Furude.
...The Furude household now consisted of only the shrine priest's family, so those seated were just the priest, his wife, and their daughter, Rika.
Rika was apparently doted on by all the village elders, and Oryou was no exception.
Attending a family council was said to be so stressful it shortened your lifespan by three days... yet Rika alone seemed immune to its oppressive atmosphere.
No matter how tense the mood around her, she paid it no mind, humming to herself as she doodled in a sketchbook.
In fact, at last night's council, Rika had been lying on her stomach with her feet tucked under Oryou's futon as if it were a kotatsu, humming and doodling contentedly in her sketchbook.
That comprised the Three Families.
...And surrounding them, row upon row, sat the Sonozaki clan's relatives and associates.
Only direct family members were granted cushions.@kEveryone else simply sat on the bare tatami in formal kneeling position.
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: ...With Empress Sonozaki at the center, they sat arrayed around her like a great serpent coiled in concentric rings.@kAnd within that arrangement, a rigid hierarchy dictated every position and seat...
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: The overwhelming majority were, in the end, Sonozaki.
...The sheer number of seats afforded to the Sonozaki family at the council that decided everything about the village made the power balance among the Three Families blindingly obvious...
The number of members from each family in attendance directly reflected their influence over Hinamizawa's governance.
``...What was discussed... at that meeting?``
Sato-san maintained his silence for a long moment before quietly beginning to speak...
``...Don't you think the monetary gifts we've been paying to the media are getting rather large?``
The one who broke the long silence was the head of the Kimiyoshi family, Kiichirou Kimiyoshi...
The Onigafuchi Defense Alliance was not a business.
...It was merely a voluntary organization advocating the cancellation of the Hinamizawa Dam Project, with no fixed source of income.
In the early days of their campaign, sizable donations had poured in, but as the struggle dragged on, those funds had been dwindling year after year...
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: ``...This isn't an era where throwing stones gets things done.@k@vS05/00/104600010. We all know the power of the media shouldn't be underestimated, and we want to continue borrowing that power.``
The expressions on the faces of those aligned with the Sonozaki family were grim.
...Because the one who had proposed leveraging the media was their own leader, Oryou Sonozaki.
This battle was not fought with force alone.
In a civilized age, there were civilized methods of warfare.
...Oryou had made that proposal and laid out a strategic direction incorporating the mass media.
Oryou's foresight had proven prescient... and the results, initially uncertain, had slowly begun to materialize.

If the physical confrontation at the construction site was a defensive tactic to delay work even by a single day,@k...then the media strategy was an offensive tactic to undermine the construction plan itself.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: Those who had initially doubted the strategy now questioned its effectiveness not at all...
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: But maintaining those media ties required a perpetual flow of enormous funds.
As long as the war chest remained full, things were fine.
The expenditures were massive, but the tangible results justified them.
...But with the struggle dragging on, circumstances were now very different from those early days.
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: Because the strategy had been personally devised by Oryou -- the head of the Sonozaki family, the apex of the Three Families -- its budget had always been treated as sacrosanct, exempt from cuts.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: ...Everyone knew it should be trimmed, but no one could bring themselves to say it.@k...That was the media expenditure.
``...What do you think, Oryou-san?``
The only person who could raise an objection to a proposal by the Sonozaki family head herself... was probably Village Chief Kimiyoshi alone.
Oryou... with an inscrutable expression, as though freed from every emotional bond of the mortal world... simply listened to Kimiyoshi's words in silence.
...Whether she was listening quietly or had no intention of listening at all, however, was impossible to tell.

``Last year, we went through a great deal of trouble getting people to accept the price increase for the bulletin.@k@vS05/18/103300012. ...They agreed to it once, but we can't keep asking for the same thing year after year.``
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: The village chief looked to the Furude family's priest and his wife for support.
...The priest wore an ambiguous expression, avoiding a direct answer.
But his wife answered without a moment's hesitation.

``That's true...@k@vS05/00/VT2_rikaM1002. The cost of the bulletin places a heavy burden on the poorer households in particular.@k@vS05/00/VT2_rikaM1003. There must be more we can cut before raising the price again.``
The 'bulletin' was, as the name implied, an official publication issued by the Onigafuchi Defense Alliance.
It outlined the Alliance's activities, principles, and resolutions, though the quality of its content was admittedly poor.
The bulletin's primary purpose was not informing readers, but rather collecting revenue by having villagers, associates, and cooperating businesses subscribe.@k...In effect, it functioned as a tax.
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: Needless to say, this was a major source of funding for the Onigafuchi Defense Alliance.
Subscription was supposedly voluntary, but in Hinamizawa it had silently become obligatory.
Even in neighboring towns, many businesses apparently subscribed reluctantly just to avoid trouble with the Alliance.
...The priest whispered to his wife that she shouldn't say anything unnecessary, but she cut him down with a single icy glance.
Withering under that glare, the priest shut his mouth.
The wife had always held the power in their relationship.@k...Because she was the one who carried the Furude bloodline.
The priest was merely an adopted son-in-law.@kAn outsider who had joined the Three Families only by marrying into the Furude name.
He was permitted a seat of considerable rank in the hierarchy, but his conspicuous lack of authority made it painfully clear that he was still treated as an outsider.
Paying no mind to her parents and their obvious differences in status, Rika continued doodling freely in her sketchbook.
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: When Oryou signaled Mion with her eyes, Mion leaned in close to her grandmother's ear.
Oryou whispered something to her in a low voice.
Mion asked two or three clarifying questions, and when Oryou nodded, Mion surveyed the room and then spoke on her grandmother's behalf...
``...The price increase for the bulletin will proceed as planned.``
For an instant, Kimiyoshi's face twisted as though he'd bitten into something bitter, but the expression vanished as if it had never been there...

``B-But, Mion-chan.@k@vS05/18/103300015. ...I'm sure you already know, but the bulletin's cost is no small burden.@k@vS05/18/103300016. If we push too hard, people on the inside will start to crack.``
``...And who, exactly, would be the first to crack?``
``Who? ...That's not what I--``
``...I'm asking who would be the first to buckle.``
Interrogated by a girl still bearing remnants of childhood, Kimiyoshi found the words lodged in his throat. He was silenced.
Mion repeated her question once more, her gaze sweeping slowly across every person in the crowded room.
To avoid being impaled by those eyes, everyone averted their gaze uneasily...
The words she spoke were, of course, Oryou's -- delivered by proxy.
So even from Mion's lips, they carried the same weight as Oryou's own.@k...But the eyes were different.
Like Oryou's, they possessed a hawkish intensity that could freeze the heart of anyone who met them, compelling submission.
...But that gaze was unmistakably Mion's own.
The day would eventually come when Mion inherited everything from Oryou and stood alone as a young, capable head of the family.
...No one wanted to mar their standing in her eyes, not even slightly.
...And that was precisely why no one dared think of Mion as just some little girl.
``...Kimiyoshi-san.@k@vS05/26/100300005. Something as trivial as a price increase for the bulletin will not shake anyone's resolve.``
Kimiyoshi grumbled softly, then made a gesture indicating he had no further objections.
``...Media-related expenditures will continue.@k@vS05/26/100300007. Should those expenses place further strain, an additional price increase for the bulletin may become unavoidable.``
Mion rendered her judgment.
...Everyone bowed their heads low, listening to her words in silence.

``In the name of Mion Sonozaki, acting head of the Sonozaki family.@k@vS05/26/100300009. By my authority, this matter is hereby decided, effective immediately.@k@vS05/26/100300010. This decision is final and not subject to appeal.``
The formulaic decree signifying her judgment was read aloud.
...In the sense that no appeal was possible, it carried a gravity that made even a court ruling pale in comparison...
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: Where a judge would strike a gavel to signify the matter was settled...
...Mion drew a large bell from within her clothing and shook it, letting it ring.
...Before the solemn sound of that bell, everyone present could only prostrate themselves...
``...What an... old-fashioned family council.``
``A young person like you would probably find it hard to believe.@k@vS05/00/104400062. ...But in these old rural areas, customs like this still run deep.``
It was exactly as Ooishi had described.
...The old system of the Three Families had effectively dissolved, and the Sonozaki family now ruled alone.
At a level that could only be called dictatorial.
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: ``...Was that the end of the meeting?``
``No, there's more.@k@vS05/00/104400064. ...I'll continue.``
When the bell's resonance finally died away, a silence sharp enough to hurt the ears descended.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: Through that silence, a man shuffled close to Mion and whispered something in her ear.
Mion quietly asked him several follow-up questions.
Once satisfied, she dismissed him with a gesture.
...The man who had spoken to Mion was a member of a yakuza organization led by Mion's biological father.
The organization controlled the entire Shishibone area and was quite well-known in the region.
...Of course, this was the underworld, so not everyone knew -- but merely flashing their insignia could settle disputes in these parts.
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: Mion's biological father -- to Oryou, her daughter's husband.
...The power of that man's gang was the very force that controlled the dark underbelly of the Onigafuchi Defense Alliance.
...And from his seat -- the highest rank below the Three Families -- he radiated an aura of quiet menace that kept everyone in his vicinity on edge...
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: Mion exchanged a look with her father.
...Was it appropriate to relay what she'd just been told directly to Oryou?
...That seemed to be what she was asking.
Her father gave a gentle but firm nod in reply.
Mion returned the nod, then leaned close to Oryou's ear and whispered something...
When she finished, Mion straightened her posture and waited for Oryou's response.
Oryou rarely showed emotion.
...So when she began to laugh, stifling the sound, everyone present could only sit in vague unease, unable to read the meaning behind her reaction.
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: ``...Well now... what an inconvenience.@k@vS05/17/102300002. ...Keh keh keh keh...!``
Oryou was laughing with such evident amusement that Kimiyoshi hesitantly ventured a question.
``...What is it, Oryou-san?``
``...If our land is like a mother to us... then this dam business is like someone threatening our mother's life, wouldn't you say?``
Kimiyoshi, unable to follow, was momentarily taken aback.
Oryou grinned widely, then addressed everyone in a voice that carried above all others.
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: ``...It seems the Minister behind the dam project... has had his grandson snatched away. He's beside himself.@k@vS05/17/102300005. Now we're even.@k@vS05/17/102300006. ...Keh keh keh keh keh!``
``...That's absurd!!!``
The kidnapping of Minister Inukai's grandson shouldn't have leaked anywhere.@k...How did they know about it?!
...The minister's grandson's kidnapping was a case about which even we knew precious little.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: How could an old rural household, over six hours from Tokyo by highway, possibly have learned of it?
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: ...Tranquil yet somehow unsettling -- Hinamizawa Village.
...And yet, somewhere in my mind, I had been arbitrarily dismissing any connection to this incident.
...That assumption had just been blown away like tissue paper.
``...How... does the Onigafuchi Defense Alliance know about this...``
Sato-san, unable to find an answer, fell into silence...
``...I'm sorry.@k@vS05/13/101300391. Please continue.``
``The grandson of the Minister of Construction... was kidnapped?@k@vS05/18/103300022. ...Oryou-san, is that true?``
An incident of that magnitude hadn't appeared in any newspaper or broadcast.
...But in Hinamizawa, no one found it strange that Oryou alone knew things the rest of the world did not.

``Our pain is the pain of losing a mother.@k@vS05/17/102300008. ...Not quite on the same level, perhaps, but losing a grandchild is pain too.@k@vS05/17/102300009. ...Wouldn't you say we're even now?``
...That final question was addressed to no one in particular.@k...It seemed spoken to seek the collective agreement of the room.

``...But land and a human child are different things.@k@vS05/17/102300013. ...I don't mind them hiding him, but I'd rather they not hurt him.@k@vS05/17/102300014. ...We're not monsters, after all. Keh keh keh...``
``Does that mean... the Onigafuchi Defense Alliance is involved in the kidnapping?!``
``...Hmm... I can't say that for certain.``
``Why not?@k@vS05/13/101300394. Their leader, Oryou, was clearly giving instructions about the treatment of the kidnapped grandson!``
``Listen, kid.@k@vS05/00/104400068. This is what the Sonozaki family always does.``
``...Always does?@k@vS05/13/101300396. What do you mean?``
...Suppose a problem arose.
...A problem unfavorable to the Sonozaki family.
If Oryou expressed 'concern' about that problem,@k...someone who had attended the family council would 'take the initiative.'
And as a result, Oryou's wish would be fulfilled.
...But neither Oryou nor anyone else would know who actually carried it out.
...Through this ingenious mechanism, the Sonozaki family had been pulling strings from the shadows for generations.
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: ``So then... the culprit is someone who attended the family council?``
``Or, it's possible there was never a culprit within the council at all.@k@vS05/00/104400070. ...Oryou herself may not have been certain that the kidnapper was among those present.``
...An unsettling, peculiar secrecy.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: ...Were they involved or not?@k...I couldn't see beyond the veil.
But this much I could say with certainty.
This kidnapping had been kept under absolute secrecy.@k...No one, regardless of their position, should have been able to learn of it.
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: The only people who could know were those connected to public security.@kThat is, us.
Or... people directly connected to the incident.
...Those affected, including the minister himself.@k...Or... the perpetrators.
...In any case... there was no way an old rural household six hours from Tokyo by expressway could possibly have known.@kThe mere fact that they knew was already deeply wrong.
...............Suspicions accumulated.@k...Piling up like falling snow.
``...That's everything Oryou said regarding the kidnapped grandson.@k@vS05/00/104400073. ...Hopefully it contained what you were looking for.``

``...The rest is your job.@k@vS05/00/104400075. ...I don't know what line of work you're in, but if you're going up against the Sonozaki family, you'd better be prepared.@k@vS05/00/104400076. Even the boss wears body armor sometimes.``
``He hasn't been lately, but there was a period when he was wearing a stab-proof vest even off duty.``
``...I see.@k@vS05/13/101300400. ...Thank you for the warning. I'll be careful.``
``...You speak awfully clean Japanese.@k@vS05/00/104400079. Are you from Tokyo, by any chance?``
``Huh?@k@vS05/13/101300402. ...What does that matter?``
An investigator was always close to danger.@k...It was best to disclose as little personal information as possible.
``About your line of work.@k@|@y@vS05/00/104400081. ...Any chance you're from the Metropolitan Police's Public Security Bureau?``
Just as I was thinking that, I was hit point-blank with a question that struck right at the heart of the matter.
I couldn't let myself hesitate here.@kThat would be as good as admitting it.

``...This is outside the scope of what the boss paid me for, but...@k@vS05/00/104400083. We sat around the same mahjong table today, so call it a freebie.@k@vS05/00/104400084. There was another topic that came up at the meeting.``
Another topic that Oryou brought up?
...And right before telling me this, Sato-san had asked if I was from the Metropolitan Police's Public Security Bureau.@k Those two things together...
...What they implied sent a violent chill down my spine.

``...So you see...@k@vS05/00/oldE00002. ...It seems that in order to investigate the kidnapping of the Minister's grandson,@k@vS05/00/oldE00003. an investigator from the Metropolitan Public Security Bureau has been sent all the way from Tokyo.``
``...An investigator from Public Security?``

``...They can't afford to make a big deal of the kidnapping,@k@vS05/00/oldE00005. so apparently the Metropolitan Public Security Bureau is handling the investigation independently.@k@vS05/00/oldE00006. And they've sent someone here to sniff around.``
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: Rika, who had shown no interest in any of the proceedings and had been drawing the entire time, suddenly stopped the moment the topic of Public Security came up and turned a curious gaze toward Oryou.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: ``...Did the Metropolitan Police come?``
``Oh my... Rika-chama knows about the Metropolitan Police?@k@vS05/00/oldE00010. ...How impressive.``
``...Who came?``
``...Does anyone here know what kind of person from Public Security was sent?``
Mion's father raised his hand slightly.
``...I hear it's some green rookie. A single one.``
``...A rookie?@k@vS05/27/100500100. Nice and fresh?``
``Hm? ...Haha!@k@vS05/00/mionF00003. Yes, that's right.@k@vS05/00/mionF00004. ...Fresh meat.``
Mion's father answered Rika's question with a wry smile.
``...Nice and fresh and fluffy?``
``Nice and fresh and fluffy.``
Rika smiled with apparent amusement.
...Why she had suddenly shown interest in this topic alone...@k...was something no one seemed to understand.
``...Does Rika-chama know anything about this man?``
``...@k@vS05/27/100500104. ...I don't know anything.``
After that curt reply, Rika burrowed back under Oryou's futon...
But this time, she didn't resume her drawing. Instead, she seemed to be deep in thought as she disappeared beneath the covers...
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: ``...What shall we do about him?@k@vS05/00/mionF00007. Mother.``
Mion's father posed the question.@k...His blunt expression seemed to say he could crush the intruder at a moment's notice, if only the order were given.
``...He came all the way to Hinamizawa.@k@vS05/17/102300017. ...Let him have a leisurely stay.``
``You mean... let him roam free...?``
Mion's father asked in response.
...Oryou gave a thin smile before answering.
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: ``...Leave him be.@k@vS05/17/102300019. ...But... if he gets too far out of line, don't hold back.@k@vS05/17/102300020. ...Keh keh keh...``
``...Is what was apparently discussed.@k@vS05/00/104400087. ...You wouldn't happen to be that rookie from the Metropolitan Police, would you?``
``...Of course not.@k@vS05/13/101300408. ...Hahahaha.``
A sensation like something cold and bristling crawling up my spine.
That family council took place last night.@k...In other words, yesterday.
...Then today, when I headed to Hinamizawa disguised as a tourist... my identity had already been exposed?
...When I recalled who was the very first person to greet me at the bus stop... I stopped breathing.
...Furude Rika -- the one who had expressed interest at the family council in the man dispatched from the Metropolitan Police.
And Furude Rika -- the one who had been waiting for me at the bus shelter the entire time.
...An unthinkable thought.@k...No -- a delusion.
When we met at that bus shelter... did Rika-chan already know who I really was?
Did she know I was Akasaka, dispatched from the Metropolitan Police, and come to meet me on purpose...?
...Then did Mayor Kimiyoshi and all the villagers who had been so kind to me... know all along and simply pretend otherwise...?
Because Oryou had instructed them not to interfere?
...Thinking about it rationally... no, I didn't believe so.
I had interacted with a great number of people.
...Some would be skilled actors, and others would be poor at hiding their feelings.
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: ...Among that many people, there should have been at least one who, knowing I was from Public Security, couldn't completely suppress their hostility.
...But I hadn't sensed anything like that at all.
But then again...
...Why had Oryou ordered them to leave me alone?
Was it because clumsy interference would only invite more scrutiny where none was warranted?
Or did she believe a green rookie like me couldn't uncover anything?
Akasaka is a coward.
...I recalled that eerie voice -- the one that seemed to have possessed Rika-chan.
...That unknown entity, no matter how I considered it, had clearly known my true identity from the very beginning.
...And what had it warned me about?
That's right.@k...It had warned me to go back to Tokyo. Immediately.
Rather than the realistic fear that the Onigafuchi Defense Alliance had uncovered my identity... the surreal dread of that unidentifiable girl's warning somehow loomed larger in my mind.
...What was that girl?@k...And what was the thing I would eventually regret?
That mysterious girl.@k...Furude Rika.
Enough.@k...I should stop thinking about it for now.
Tomorrow, first thing in the morning, I would report everything I'd heard tonight to headquarters and await further instructions.
There was no denying that the possibility of the Onigafuchi Defense Alliance's involvement in this case had grown considerably.
...And with my identity potentially compromised, my situation had become dangerous.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: ...Though given that headquarters was currently stretched to the breaking point, they might not be able to spare any support.
``...No, nothing like that.@k@vS05/13/VX_akasaka_0014. I'm simply tracking the perpetrator of a kidnapping case that occurred in Tokyo.``
Striving to maintain a calm facade, I gave the answer I had prepared in advance for this type of question.
Sato-san stole a brief glance at my face, replied with a vague '...That so?' and asked nothing further.
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: ``...That everything?@k@vS05/00/104400089. If you'd like, I can drop you off wherever.``
``Ah... thank you.@k@vS05/13/101300410. The station where we originally met, please.``
A rural highway where even streetlights were scarce.
...I couldn't see anything.
Nothing was visible beyond the strip of asphalt lit by the headlights.
Beyond the light -- was it merely darkness?@kOr was something lurking there, watching?
...What a miserable assignment, I thought.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: ...I wanted to go back to Tokyo.
For some reason, the voice of that girl warning me to go back to Tokyo still lingered in my head, refusing to fade...
